column1: # DOLCHEO+ encoding TSV — schema per canon/governance/writing-conventions.md
# D, O, L, C rows (6 cols): type
# Tab-separated. Order within file: D/O/L/C/H first, then O-open at end.
# Session: 2026-05-29 audio bench parity (slice-1 follow-on: duration header, no-store cache fix, shared nav, parallel bench)

column2: Worker forwards container duration as X-Transcode-Duration
column3: The container already ffprobes duration (X-Audio-Duration) but the Worker dropped it. The Worker now captures it into the transcode meta, persists it in R2 customMetadata, and emits X-Transcode-Duration on both MISS and HIT. Length is preserved by transcode, so the source and every variant share one value; the passthrough source (no duration header) borrows the length any transcoded tile reports. Extends the audio response contract in canon/planning/2026-05-29-audio-worker-path.md.
column4: container/server.mjs already emitted X-Audio-Duration; worker meta omitted it. Added capture + R2 persist + emit on MISS and HIT; bun run typecheck clean, 86 tests pass.
column5: Client-side <audio> loadedmetadata duration (rejected — the bench reads proxy headers, not client-computed values, per image-bench parity); leave length unsurfaced (rejected — length is the metric a packager cares about and it was surfaced nowhere).
column6: working — needs redeploy; additive header so versions upload suffices

column2: Audio bench is an official fourth route; cross-page nav is one identical four-link set
column3: /bench/audio joins /film, /bench, /casestudy as a first-class demo route. The header menu is now the same four-link set on every page — Film / Image bench / Audio bench / Case study — with only aria-current differing per page. demo-page.test.ts is extended to require the /bench/audio link on all four pages, turning the nav-drift risk the three-page demo-spa-routing doc flagged into an enforced invariant. Extends canon/planning/2026-05-29-demo-spa-routing.md (which assumed three pages, one menu).
column4: All four page modules in the PAGES guard array; new links-to-all-four-routes assertion plus the existing exactly-one-current assertion pass; 86 tests green.
column5: Leave the audio bench unlinked (rejected — orphaned page); per-page bespoke navs (rejected — the exact drift the doc warned about).

column2: Audio bench parity model: parallel, run-on-change, source-as-sorted-tile, target+measured
column3: The audio bench mirrors the image bench. No Run button — it runs on change of source/preset/codec/quality and on load. All probes (source + each selected quality) fire at once via Promise.all rather than a sequential chain, since the proxy is built for concurrency, with a run-generation guard discarding superseded runs. The source renders as a tile in the same grid as the variants, tagged source, and all tiles sort by delivered size descending (source on top, then high to medium to low). A source-info summary line shows name/size/length/type. The q checkbox labels and a legend show the recipe TARGET bitrate (8/16/32 kbps) while each tile shows the live MEASURED bitrate alongside, so target and actual sit together.
column4: Shipped; guard invariants hold (one <script>, all getElementById ids present, run-btn and baselineWrap refs removed); 86 tests pass.
column5: Sequential fetch chain plus an explicit Run button (rejected — needless serialization on a concurrent proxy and an extra click); separate baseline panel outside the grid (rejected — the size comparison reads better with the source as the top tile).
column6: working — visible after redeploy (bench HTML is worker-served)

column2: Immutable responses freeze the browser-cached header; measurement probes must use cache:no-store
column3: The audio bench showed low quality as always-MISS and super fast. Root cause was client-side, not the cache: /audio responses are Cache-Control public, max-age=31536000, immutable (correct for content-addressed output), so the browser served repeat measurement probes from its disk cache and replayed the X-Transcode-Cache value frozen on first fetch — a later server HIT still read as the original MISS, and the disk-cache serve is what made it instant. Fix: the bench measurement fetch uses cache:no-store so every probe reaches the worker. The <audio> players keep normal caching for playback; the immutable header is right for real consumers and was left as-is.
column4: Live curl against prod: q=low returns HIT on every request (43929-byte output); Cache-Control confirmed immutable. Server cache verified working; only the in-browser bench saw the stale header. no-store fix shipped, 86 tests pass.
column5: Drop or relax the immutable header (rejected — correct and valuable for real CDN/browser caching); cache:reload (works but still stores the response — no-store is cleaner for a pure measurement probe).
column6: permanent — applies to any in-browser measurement of an immutable response

column2: Per-tile sample rate omitted: Opus decodes at 48 kHz regardless of coded rate
column3: Opus always decodes at 48 kHz (RFC 7845) no matter the coded 8/16/24 kHz target, and ffprobe on the Ogg stream reports 48000. Showing that per tile would contradict the q-target legend (which states the coded 8/16/24 kHz). So tiles show measured bitrate + channels but not sample rate; the coded rate lives only in the legend, as a target. The low/medium/high size difference is governed by bitrate, not the reported sample rate.
column4: Prior OpusHead experiment (output always 48 kHz decode); container ffprobe reports 48000 for Ogg Opus regardless of coded rate.
column5: Show ffprobe 48000 per tile (rejected — contradicts the legend and misleads); fabricate the coded rate client-side (rejected — not measured).
column6: working — resolved properly only by the H row below (surface OpusHead coded rate)

column2: Redeploy to surface duration; optional X-Transcode-CodedRate follow-up
column3: All bench + duration changes need a worker redeploy to be visible (bench HTML is worker-served). The AudioContainer DO migration was already applied by a one-time full wrangler deploy in slice 1, and X-Transcode-Duration is an additive header, so an ordinary versions upload is enough this time. Optional next step: surface the OpusHead coded rate as X-Transcode-CodedRate so the bench can build the q-to-rate mapping from live data instead of the static legend, which also makes a truthful per-tile coded-rate stat possible (resolves the sample-rate constraint above).

column4: Mirror BSB/LibriVox bench samples to R2 for production
column5: The audio bench defaults point at openbible.com (BSB, Bob Souer narration) and archive.org (LibriVox) — community hosts. Both are public domain, but for a production demo the samples should be mirrored to an R2 bucket the project controls, to avoid hotlinking, rate limits, or upstream churn. Closes by copying the five sample files to R2 and repointing the dropdown.
